Learnings: What you learned from the data

Hypothesis: Running a daily discussion prompt will increase weekly active member rate by 15%.
Result: Weekly active rate increased 18% (from 31% to 49%). Reply rate was highest for opinion-based prompts vs factual ones.
Learnings: Daily prompts work. Opinion-based questions get 3x more replies than knowledge-based ones. Engagement dipped on weekends — members skip Saturday/Sunday prompts.
Next Step: Make it permanent. Drop weekend prompts (M-F only). Switch to 80% opinion-based, 20% knowledge-based questions.

Experiment Name: Welcome DM Automation — new member onboarding
Hypothesis: A 5-day automated DM sequence will increase 30-day retention by 20%.
Result: 30-day retention improved 11% (from 62% to 73%). Day-2 DM (profile setup prompt) had highest click-through rate.
Learnings: Automation helps but personal touch matters more — members who replied to any DM had 85% retention vs 58% who didn't engage.
Next Step: Add a human check-in call on day 4 for all new members. A/B test automated vs human-welcome for 1 month.

Hypothesis: Featuring one member per month in a spotlight post will increase NPS promoter score by 10%.
Result: NPS promoters stayed at 52% (no significant change). However, featured members showed 40% increase in weekly posts after their spotlight.
Learnings: Spotlight doesn't move overall NPS much but strongly impacts the featured member's engagement. Good for retention of active members, not for broader sentiment.
Next Step: Expand to bi-weekly spotlights. Add a nomination system so members can nominate each other. Measure impact on retention rate instead of NPS.

Experiment Name: Tiered Referral Rewards — 3 tiers instead of 1
Hypothesis: Tiered rewards (3/5/10 referrals) will increase total referrals by 25% compared to flat 1-month-free reward.
Result: Total referrals increased 32% (from 22 to 29). The 3-referral tier had 80% of all redemptions. The 10-referral tier was never reached.
Learnings: Tiered rewards work. 3-referral level is the sweet spot. 5 and 10 feel too far away. People shared their link most in the first week after joining.
Next Step: Simplify to 2 tiers: 3 referrals = 1 month free, 5 referrals = 2 months + exclusive access. Test for 6 weeks.

Hypothesis: Rotating event times across 3 time zones will increase event attendance by 40% and improve international member NPS.
Result: Overall attendance increased 52%. PST events averaged 18 attendees (was 6). UK events averaged 11. ET events stayed at 22 (was 20). International members' satisfaction up 0.8 points.
Learnings: Time zone rotation is a clear win. ET still gets most attendance but PST growth was dramatic. UK attendance is lower but consistent — worth keeping.
Next Step: Add a monthly APAC-friendly time slot (8am ET = 8pm Singapore). Keep rotating ET/PST/UK weekly.

Experiment Name: Free Trial — 7-day premium access for free members
Hypothesis: Offering a 7-day free trial of the paid tier will convert 15% of free members and increase overall conversion rate by 5%.
Result: Free trial started by 34 free members. 8 converted to paid (23.5% conversion). Overall conversion rate increased from 8% to 10.2%.
Learnings: Free trial converts well. Members who used the trial engaged 3x more. Best conversion came from members who'd been free for 30-60 days (not brand new).
Next Step: Make free trial permanent but limit eligibility to free members who've been active for 30+ days. Target 12% conversion rate in next quarter.

Experiment Name: Weekly Digest — curated top posts email
Hypothesis: A Friday weekly digest email will increase weekly active member rate by 10% by surfacing content members might miss.
Result: Weekly active rate increased 7%. Open rate was 68%. Click-through rate was 14%. 12 lapsed members re-engaged after receiving the digest.
Learnings: Digest is effective for re-engagement, especially for lapsed members. Working members who don't browse daily appreciate the curation.
Next Step: Add a member-wins section to the digest. A/B test Monday vs Friday send day. Measure impact on both engagement AND churn reduction.

Experiment Name: New Member Orientation Call — monthly live walkthrough
Hypothesis: A monthly live orientation call will increase 14-day retention by 15% and reduce time-to-first-post.
Result: 14-day retention increased 9%. Average time-to-first-post dropped from 4.2 days to 2.8 days. Average 12 attendees per session.
Learnings: Orientation calls work. Most effective component was the live Q&A — members who asked questions had highest retention. Having a current member co-host added social proof.
Next Step: Expand to bi-weekly. Add a structured 30-min format (15 min walkthrough + 15 min open Q&A). Recruit a member co-host rotation.

Hypothesis: Creating a formal ambassador application process will increase active ambassadors from 3 to 6 within 2 months.
Result: 5 new applications received. 3 accepted as ambassadors. 2 waitlisted for next round. Application quality was high — all applicants were active members for 90+ days.
Learnings: Formal process attracts quality candidates. The application itself filtered out people who weren't serious. Current ambassadors helped review candidates — their input was invaluable.
Next Step: Run another application round at the start of Q3. Add a referral bonus for ambassadors who recommend a new ambassador who's accepted.

Experiment Name: Member Directory by Expertise — searchable database
Hypothesis: A searchable member directory sorted by expertise areas will increase 1-on-1 connection requests by 30%.
Result: Directory added. 64 members filled in their expertise tags. Connection requests increased 24%. Most-used tags: Content Marketing (42%), Community Building (38%), SaaS Growth (31%).
Learnings: Adoption was high but data quality varied — 30% of entries were vague (just listed 'marketing'). Structured dropdown tags worked much better than free-text fields.
Next Step: Add required dropdown fields for new members during onboarding. Backfill existing members with a monthly prompt to update their profile. Target 80% directory completion rate.
